description: Assign or get information about beam constitutive models. This command allows one to assign a constitutive model to a specified beam-type element, query properties and states of constitutive models, and assign and query the integration scheme for plastic elements.

6.0.reason: Not listed in the official FLAC3D 6.0 command indexes.

7.0.reason: Not listed in the official FLAC3D 7.0 contents command index.

keywords.name: assign
keywords.description: Assign the specified constitutive model to all beam elements in the range. If a constitutive model has already been assigned, the existing one is replaced with the new one. If an elastic or plastic model is replaced by an elastic one, the nodal forces acting on the element remain the same. If a plastic model is replaced by another plastic model, both the integration scheme and the stress at each integration point are preserved. Replacing an elastic model with a plastic model is not yet implemented. Properties of the newly assigned constitutive model take their default values and should be set with the structure beam property command. The integration scheme for plastic models is set with the structure beam cmodel plastic-integration command.

keywords.name: assign-elastic
keywords.description: assign the isotropic elastic model (see the Beam Elastic Constitutive Model description).

keywords.name: assign-mohr-coulomb
keywords.description: assign the Mohr-Coulomb elasto-plasticity model in one-dimensional mode (see the Mohr-Coulomb model description). Plastic behavior is supported by the Euler-Bernoulli finite element.

keywords.name: assign-strain-softening
keywords.description: assign the strain-softening/hardening Mohr-Coulomb elasto-plasticity model in one-dimensional mode (see the Mohr-Coulomb strain-softening model description). The softening properties vary over the integration points.

keywords.name: assign-von-mises
keywords.description: assign the Von-Mises elasto-plasticity model in one-dimensional mode (see the Von-Mises model description). Plastic behavior is supported by the Euler-Bernoulli finite element.

keywords.name: list
keywords.description: List information about beam constitutive models. Sub-keywords are names, plastic-integration, properties, and states.

keywords.name: list-names
keywords.description: Output available beam constitutive model keywords and full names. Output is restricted to the model named s if supplied.

keywords.name: list-plastic-integration
keywords.description: Output the plastic-integration data for elements in the range. Data for all integration points is listed unless integration-point <ind> is specified, in which case only the data for integration point ind is listed.

keywords.name: list-properties
keywords.description: Output the property list for each beam constitutive model. Output is restricted to the model named s if supplied.

keywords.name: list-states
keywords.description: Output the state flags for each beam constitutive model. Output is restricted to the model named s if supplied.

keywords.name: plastic-integration
keywords.description: Assign the integration scheme consisting of the cross-sectional shape and size and the integration-point layout. Both sub-keywords cross-section and layout must be specified together. There is no default; this command must be executed before cycling the model. The integration scheme cannot be modified after cycling has occurred. When the integration scheme is set, the beam constitutive-model properties are reset to their defaults, so model properties should be set AFTER setting the integration scheme.

keywords.name: plastic-integration-cross-section
keywords.description: Specify the cross-sectional shape. circular <R> assigns a circular cross section with radius R. rectangular <ly> <lz> assigns a rectangular cross section with side lengths ly (y-direction) and lz (z-direction).

keywords.name: plastic-integration-layout
keywords.description: Number of integration points along the beam length (nx) and across the cross section. For a rectangular cross section: i2 = ny (integration points in y), i3 = nz (integration points in z). For a circular cross section: i2 = nr (radial integration points), i3 = ntheta (circumferential integration points). Allowable ranges: nx in {1..12}; ny, nz in {2..12}; nr in {2,4,6,8}; ntheta in {7,9,11}. At least two integration points along the element axis and across the rectangular section, or seven in the circumferential direction of the circular section, are required to resist bending. Suggested minimal layout: (nx=ny=nz=2) for rectangular, (nx=2, nr=2, ntheta=7) for circular.
